meta.title: 4.5 Edge Caching
meta.description: Caching at the CDN edge, stale-while-revalidate, edge compute, and personalized content at the edge.

blocks.prompt: A product page on an e-commerce site is 90% identical for all users but 10% personalized (cart count, recommended products). Can you cache this page at the CDN edge? How would you handle the personalized parts?

blocks.content:
## Edge caching strategies ### Cache-Control headers The primary mechanism for controlling CDN caching: ``` Cache-Control: public, max-age=3600 # Cache for 1 hour Cache-Control: private, no-cache # Don't cache at CDN Cache-Control: public, s-maxage=60 # CDN caches 60s, browser different Cache-Control: public, stale-while-revalidate=300 # Serve stale for 5min while refreshing ``` ### Stale-while-revalidate The most powerful edge caching strategy. When the cached content expires: 1. Serve the stale content immediately (fast!) 2. In the background, fetch fresh content from origin 3. Update the cache for subsequent requests **Result:** Users never wait for origin. Content is at most one TTL period stale. Perfect for content that changes periodically but does not need real-time freshness. ### Edge-Side Includes (ESI) Assemble pages at the edge from cacheable fragments. The page template is cached; dynamic fragments are fetched separately. ```html
``` Cache the product content for hours. Fetch cart-count fresh (or cache with short TTL per user). ### Personalization at the edge **Strategy 1: Client-side personalization.** Cache the generic page at the edge. Use JavaScript to fetch and render personalized elements after page load. **Strategy 2: Edge compute.** Run code at the CDN edge (Cloudflare Workers, Lambda@Edge) to inject personalized content based on cookies or geolocation. **Strategy 3: Vary header.** Cache different versions based on request characteristics: `Vary: Accept-Language` caches one version per language. ### What NOT to cache at the edge - Authenticated API responses (unless using Vary on auth token — creates too many variants) - Real-time data (stock prices, live scores) - Write operations (POST, PUT, DELETE) - Content with high cardinality personalization (unique per user)

blocks.question: Your marketing team launches a flash sale and product prices change. 200 CDN edge servers worldwide have the old prices cached with a 1-hour TTL. What is your fastest response?
blocks.answer: **Fastest: Instant purge + stale-while-revalidate.** (1) Call the CDN's purge API to invalidate all product page caches globally. Fastly purges in <150ms; CloudFront in 5-10 minutes. (2) If you use stale-while-revalidate, users get the old price only until the background refresh completes (seconds). (3) Long-term fix: use versioned URLs for price-sensitive content, or reduce TTL for product pages to 60 seconds with stale-while-revalidate=3600 (serve stale for up to an hour, but refresh every 60s). This gives near-instant freshness with the resilience of long caching.

blocks.content: Stale-while-revalidate is the most powerful edge caching strategy — users never wait for origin. Handle personalization via client-side rendering, edge compute, or ESI fragments. Cache generic content aggressively and fetch personalized elements separately.

blocks.prompt: Module 4.6 is the checkpoint — design a complete caching strategy for an e-commerce product catalog, combining all levels from in-process to edge.
